rationale: Both candidates converge on the same well-grounded core finding (default_components duplicates the pipeline's own _dit_modules metadata, citing matching file:line evidence in pipeline_sdxl.py/registry.py/module_collector.py) that echoes the spirit, if not the exact letter, of dsocek's GT concern about a fragile hardcoded naming/mapping list — neither hits dsocek's specific packed_modules_mapping suggestion, capping recall for both. X is more concise and non-redundant, pairing that finding with a distinct, specific second issue (text-encoder LoRA silently no-op) and a calibrated approve-level verdict matching the actual LGTM/merged outcome. Y pads its output with trivial 'validated' sanity checks and splits essentially one idea into two overlapping findings (#1 and #2), diluting precision/actionability slightly, though its integration-test-coverage point is a useful distinct addition.
